summary_one_line: A parsed cHRM green-x chromaticity value driven out of [0, PNG_FP_1=100000] so the greenx>PNG_FP_1 guard takes its true (reject) branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input must reach png_XYZ_from_xy with a green chromaticity field whose fixed-point value satisfies greenx<0 OR greenx>PNG_FP_1 (100000 = 0x000186A0), i.e. the multi-byte greenx operand must be pushed above the 100000 magnitude threshold so the line-1288 guard takes its true side and returns 1.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile_cmplog winner reaches line 1288 and takes the true side (W shows T=1 F=0 at L1288 while the loser shows T=0 F=40), resolves the branch in 9/10 trials, and accumulates 56 hits versus the loser's 3 — its chromaticity bytes land greenx outside the [0,PNG_FP_1] window.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser only ever takes the false side at L1288 (T=0 F=40), blocks in 8/10 trials, and never drives greenx above the 100000 threshold; it stalls 21.10h on the branch with only 3 hits, so its green chromaticity stays inside the in-range window.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers carry the same input-to-state substitution mutator (cmplog and vpc are identical except for value_profile), so the comparison-solving mutator is held constant; the winning technique is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient. The comparison greenx>PNG_FP_1 is a magnitude inequality against the multi-byte constant 100000 (0x000186A0) that literal-substitution mutation cannot directly solve, but the CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance buckets reward inputs whose greenx operand creeps progressively closer to and past 100000, giving the gradient that lets vpc overshoot the threshold where plain cmplog cannot.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the inequality guard greenx>PNG_FP_1 at png.c:1288 whose true side is globally blocked.
The single decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog beating cmplog with the technique delta being value_profile.
The winner resolves 9/10 and the loser blocks 8/10 with a large hitcount gap.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=56  loser=3

evidence_trail.claim: The winner takes the true side of the line-1288 guard while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: T=1 F=0  T=0 F=40  if (xy->greenx < 0 || xy->greenx > PNG_FP_1) return 1;  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser stalls far longer on the branch than the winner.
value_profile_cmplog's feedback adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of the edge and CMP-buffer signals.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog and value_profile_cmplog share the same I2SRandReplace mutator stack, so I2S is not the differentiator.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient, not I2SRandReplace, so lineage dash-row verification is not the relevant signal here. The decisive delta is value_profile (DECISIVE PAIRS Pair 1), and both involved fuzzers (cmplog, value_profile_cmplog) carry identical I2SRandReplace mutators per MECHANISM CONTEXT, so I2S is held constant and the only difference is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback; no I2S lineage check is mandated for this mechanism.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on an out-of-range multi-byte greenx>100000 magnitude inequality where value_profile_cmplog does NOT outperform cmplog (no dose-response separation), which would show the CMP_MAP gradient is not what drives the divergence.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact greenx byte offsets that the winner pushes out of range are not isolated in the BYTE DIFF (the cHRM chromaticity field is buried in a later chunk), so the attribution rests on the value_profile delta plus the inequality-against-100000 source shape rather than a pinpointed seed-byte gradient.
